method_summary: Introduces character distribution signatures for AI text detection, creating a 'Wall of Separation' between human and AI text based on domain-specialized vs. global character patterns. Introduces the Letter Distribution Score (LD-Score) with low correlation to perplexity methods.
key_innovation: MDTA benchmark (Models-Domains-Temperatures-Adversarials) comprising 642,274 prompt-aligned samples across 4 models, 5 domains, 3 temperature settings, and 3 adversarial strategies.

key_results.improvement_metrics: consistent improvements in AUROC and F1 when integrated with DNA-DetectLLM, Binoculars and FastDetectGPT

best_result: Consistent improvements in AUROC and F1, particularly pronounced gains in specialized domains
title: Beyond Perplexity: Character Distribution Signatures and the MDTA Benchmark for AI Text Detection
